description_long: This is a rectangular, three-story office building with a flat, concrete roof and a facade clad in light gray brickwork. The front features two rows of large, square windows on the upper floors and a single door with a window and a large ground-floor window, suggesting a main entrance and lobby area. The side and rear elevations are more austere, with fewer windows and a consistent brick pattern, emphasizing functionality over ornamentation. Horizontal bands of darker material separate each floor, adding subtle visual interest and structural definition. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the building's overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements. The structure is typical of mid- to late-20th-century urban office architecture, designed for practicality and efficient use of space.
description: A three-story, rectangular office building with a flat roof, light gray brick facade, and symmetrical rows of square windows. The front features a main entrance and large windows, while the sides and rear are more minimal.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a single door, a large ground-floor window, and two rows of square windows above, all set in a light gray brick facade.
description_view_1: The left side view displays two evenly spaced windows on the middle floor, with the rest of the facade being plain brickwork and no visible entrance.
description_view_2: The rear view features a single door at the ground level and no windows on the upper floors, emphasizing a utilitarian back facade.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with two square windows on the middle floor and otherwise unadorned brickwork.
attribute_window_pattern: Symmetrical, horizontally aligned rows of square windows on the front and sides, with regular spacing and repetition; minimal fenestration on the rear.
